rationale: The cited article appears as a single background reference in the future-research discussion, where the authors note that adding a third option can sometimes produce a compromise effect (middle-option preference). It is not used to build the focal hypotheses, mechanism, study design logic, measures, or analyses of the closing-the-gap effect. The citing paper does not adopt or test the cited paper's choice models, so the role is a passing contextual mention rather than a meaningful intellectual dependency.
